name: Despoblado de Pardos y Mirador de Santa Cruz — circular desde Acered
region_intro: Mountain biking around Acered offers a diverse landscape characterized by significant elevation changes and varied terrain, suitable for different skill levels. The region features a mix of open areas and routes that traverse through natural settings, providing a range of mountain biking experiences. Trails often include ascents and descents, reflecting the area's topographical features. The landscape supports both longer endurance rides and shorter, more focused excursions. Best mountain bike trails around Acered The most popular mountain bike trail is Despoblado de Pardos y Mirador de Santa Cruz — circular desde Acered, a difficult 37.7 miles (60.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 49 minutes to complete, offering panoramic views from the Mirador de Santa Cruz. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Drinking Water Fountain loop from Fuentes de Jiloca, a moderate 29.2 miles (47.0 km) path. This route features rolling terrain and passes through rural landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Castillo de Ayud – Daroca loop from Apeadero de Villafeliche, a 72.2 miles (116.2 km) trail leading through historical areas and varied countryside, often completed in about 9 hours 26 minutes. Mountain biking around Acered is defined by its diverse elevation profiles, rural landscapes, and opportunities for long-distance rides. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to advanced mountain biking Acered. The routes in Acered are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 70 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Acered's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Acered?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails documented on komoot around Acered, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly mountain bike trails in Acered?
A: While the region is characterized by significant elevation changes, many routes are classified as moderate. There are no trails specifically rated as 'easy' in the immediate vicinity, but moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31505533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Drinking Water Fountain loop from Fuentes de Jiloca&lt;/a&gt; offer rolling terrain that can be suitable for riders with some experience.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Acered?
A: Experienced mountain bikers will find several challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986866402/despoblado-de-pardos-y-mirador-de-santa-cruz-circular-desde-acered" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Despoblado de Pardos y Mirador de Santa Cruz — circular desde Acered&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 60.7 km trail with significant elevation, offering panoramic views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38149826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Santa Cruz – Bello Village Center loop from Orcajo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 45.6 km long and also rated difficult.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes around Acered?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39837936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Ayud – Daroca loop from Apeadero de Villafeliche&lt;/a&gt; stands out. This difficult trail covers 116.2 km and leads through historical areas and varied countryside, typically taking over 9 hours to complete.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Acered?
A: The terrain around Acered is diverse, featuring significant elevation changes, rural landscapes, and a mix of open areas and natural settings. You'll encounter both ascents and descents, reflecting the area's topographical features, with routes traversing varied countryside.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Acered area?
A: Many of the mountain bike trails around Acered are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986866402/despoblado-de-pardos-y-mirador-de-santa-cruz-circular-desde-acered" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Despoblado de Pardos y Mirador de Santa Cruz — circular desde Acered&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23752740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calatayud – Maluenda loop from Morata de Jiloca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some interesting places to see along the mountain bike trails in Acered?
A: While riding, you can explore several points of interest. Consider visiting the historic settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5057738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villafeliche&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4473224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of the Martyrs&lt;/a&gt;, or the architectural &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7276808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Railway Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features natural landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4644136" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Descent of the Barranco de San Blas de Miedes&lt;/a&gt; gorge and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4591317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Murero&lt;/a&gt; mountain pass.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Acered?
A: The mountain biking routes in Acered are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 70 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse elevation profiles and rural landscapes.

Q: Is Acered suitable for mountain biking in winter?
A: Acered experiences cold winters, and while some trails may remain accessible, conditions can vary significantly. It's advisable to check local weather forecasts and trail conditions before heading out, as snow or ice could make certain routes challenging or impassable. The best time to visit for mountain biking is typically during the warmer, drier months.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass by places with amenities like pubs or restaurants?
A: Many routes in Acered traverse rural landscapes and pass through or near small villages. While specific pubs are not highlighted on every trail, routes that connect towns or settlements, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23752740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calatayud – Maluenda loop from Morata de Jiloca&lt;/a&gt;, are more likely to offer opportunities for refreshments or a meal.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Acered?
A: The diverse landscape of Acered is generally best enjoyed during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the weather is more stable. Summer can be hot, especially in open areas, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potential for snow or ice at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any hidden gem mountain bike trails in Acered?
A: While the most popular routes are well-known, the region offers 16 different tours, many of which are less frequented. Exploring some of the moderate trails not listed as 'most popular' can lead to quieter experiences and a chance to discover less-traveled paths through the varied countryside.
tips:
The story tells that the Cid slept in the town of Pardos. Apart from a long and exciting history that would not fit in this space, the abandoned town has a cyclopean wall, two Islamic towers and a legendary lagoon, but it never had an access road, which is why it was abandoned.

Great video of Despoblados y Abandonados to know the history of Pardos: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0CO9KNcvFxE&t=200s&ab_channel=DespobladosyAbandonados

Relatively close to the popular Gallocanta Lagoon is the Guialguerrero Lagoon, a lagoon surrounded by vegetation with its own spring so it never runs dry. Located near an old hermitage, it makes us intuit the existence of an old town with the name of Guialguerrero.
It is the highest viewpoint in the Campo de Daroca region, 1423m (Pico de Santa Cruz)
